nameMeanings.name: Botterell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Buttery
meanings: from Anglo-Norman French boterie ‘buttery’ (from Late Latin botaria, a derivative of bota ‘cask’), hence a metonymic occupational name for the keeper of a buttery. The term originally denoted a store for liquor but soon came to mean a store for provisions in general.

variants.name: Butter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Arno, Gerhard, Mathias, Reinhart.
Dutch and German: occupational name for a dairyman or seller of butter, from Middle Dutch butter and Middle High German buter.
English: occupational name for a dairyman, a shortened form of Middle English buterer ‘maker or seller of butter’. Alternatively, it may be a nickname from Middle English buter(e) ‘butter’.

variants.name: Botterill
meanings: 1 Norman, English: locative name from Les Bottereaux (Eure, Normandy), a place-name which apparently meant ‘the toads’. The surname is found in both a singular form Boterel and a plural form Boterels, Botereaus, which has developed to Buttress. Some of the post-medieval bearers may belong under (2).

2 Norman, English: occupational name from Anglo-Norman French * boterel, a diminutive form of Old French * boter ‘an officer who looks after the (wine) butts’. Both terms are derivatives of Old French butte, bote ‘wine-butt, cask’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Botterell was born in 1238, in Cornwall, England, her father, Reginald de Botreaux, was 21 and her mother, Margaret De Whitmore, was 17. She had at least 1 son with John De Trelawney or Trelony. She died on 19 December 1327, in Yorkshire, England, at the age of 89.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Botterell est née en 1238, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Reginald de Botreaux, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Margaret De Whitmore, avait 17 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec John De Trelawney or Trelony. Elle est décédée le 19 décembre 1327, à Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 89 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Botterell 1238 in Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Reginald de Botreaux 21 und ihre Mutter Margaret De Whitmore 17. Sie hatte mit John De Trelawney or Trelony mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 19. Dezember 1327 im Alter von 89 Jahren in Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Botterell è nata il nel 1238 nel Cornwall, England, sua padre, Reginald de Botreaux, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Margaret De Whitmore, aveva 17 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con John De Trelawney or Trelony. Morì il 19 dicembre 1327, nel Yorkshire, England, all'età di 89 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Botterell(이)가 1238년 영국 코른월에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Reginald de Botreaux(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret De Whitmore(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 John De Trelawney or Trelony(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1327년 12월 19일 영국 요르크실르에서 89세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Botterell родилась в 1238 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, её отцу по имени Reginald de Botreaux было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret De Whitmore, 17. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от John De Trelawney or Trelony. Она умерла 19 декабря 1327 г. в местности еркшир, Англия в возрасте 89 лет.
